meaning: A brief reply confirming receipt of a message or information
definition: A brief reply confirming receipt of a message or information
example: Please send an acknowledgement once you receive the brief.

meaning: A file sent along with an email message
definition: Emotional dependency or connection to a person, place, or thing
example: Please find the report as an attachment to this email.

meaning: An automatic response sent when someone is unavailable
definition: An automatic response sent when someone is unavailable
example: She set an auto-reply message while on vacation.

meaning: Blind Carbon Copy; sending a copy to recipients without others knowing
definition: Blind Carbon Copy; sending a copy to recipients without others knowing
example: He BCCed the HR department on the sensitive message.

meaning: Carbon Copy; sending a copy of an email to additional recipients
definition: Carbon Copy; sending a copy of an email to additional recipients
example: I will CC the manager so she is aware of our discussion.

meaning: The ability of an email to successfully reach the recipient's inbox
definition: The ability of an email to successfully reach the recipient's inbox
example: Poor email deliverability results in messages being marked as spam.

meaning: To insert content such as an image, video, or link directly into a message
definition: To insert content such as an image, video, or link directly into a message
example: He embedded the video link in the email so recipients could watch immediately.

meaning: A message sent after a meeting or conversation to summarise actions or next steps
definition: A message sent after a meeting or conversation to summarise actions or next steps
example: She sent a follow-up email recapping the decisions made in the meeting.
Please forward the invitation to your colleague.

meaning: A clickable link in a digital message that redirects to a webpage or file
definition: A link from a hypertext file or document to another location or file.
example: Click the hyperlink in the email to access the registration form.

meaning: A tag applied to an email to categorise and organise messages
definition: A tag applied to an email to categorise and organise messages
example: She uses labels like 'urgent' and 'invoices' to sort her inbox.

meaning: A collection of email addresses used to send messages to multiple people at once
definition: A collection of email addresses used to send messages to multiple people at once
example: Subscribe to our mailing list for weekly updates.

meaning: A regularly distributed email containing news and updates
definition: A regularly distributed email containing news and updates
example: The company sends a newsletter to all clients every Friday.

meaning: An automatic email reply informing senders that the recipient is away
definition: An automatic email reply informing senders that the recipient is away
example: He set an out-of-office message for the duration of his leave.

meaning: A fraudulent attempt to obtain sensitive information via deceptive emails
definition: The fraudulent practice of sending emails purporting to be from reputable companies in order to induce individuals to reveal personal information.
example: The phishing email appeared to come from the bank but was fake.

meaning: Responding to an email so that all original recipients receive the response
definition: Responding to an email so that all original recipients receive the response
example: Do not reply-all unless your response is relevant to everyone.

meaning: A block of text appended to the end of an email with the sender's details
definition: A block of text appended to the end of an email with the sender's details
example: His email signature includes his phone number and job title.

meaning: Unsolicited or irrelevant messages sent in bulk, especially by email
definition: Unsolicited or irrelevant messages sent in bulk, especially by email
example: The promotional newsletter ended up in his spam folder.

meaning: The brief heading of an email that summarises its content
definition: The brief heading of an email that summarises its content
example: A clear subject line helps recipients prioritise their inbox.

meaning: A pre-designed format or structure used to create documents or messages
definition: A pre-designed layout or format used as a starting point for content.
example: Use the email template for consistent brand communication.

meaning: To remove oneself from an email mailing list
definition: To remove oneself from an email mailing list
example: Click the link at the bottom of the email to unsubscribe.
